---

# /split

> When the d365-ce spec touches another agent's domain (integration / reporting / d365-fo), emit one `handoff.v1` per target agent into `projects/{p}/_handoffs/` so the downstream agent can scaffold its own feature folder + skeleton spec.

## Usage

```
/split [--feature <slug>] [--project <name>] [--targets <comma-separated>]
```

`--targets` is auto-detected from spec body if omitted. Manual override is allowed (e.g., `--targets integration,reporting`).

## When to use

After `/review --approve`. Inputs that trigger split:
- Spec mentions an Azure Function or external API call → `integration`
- Spec mentions a Power BI dashboard or CE SSRS report → `reporting`
- Spec mentions an F&O entity or DMF flow → `d365-fo`

## Execution flow

1. Read `projects/{p}/d365-ce/features/{f}/spec.md`.
2. Run keyword + structural scan against the spec body to detect cross-agent surface area.
3. For each target agent, render a `handoff.v1` JSON document with:
   - `sourceAgent: d365-ce`, `sourceFeature: {slug}`
   - `targetAgent`, `targetArtifact: spec-seed`
   - `payload`: relevant spec excerpt + cross-references back to the d365-ce spec
   - `acceptanceCriteria`: subset of CE spec FR/AC tagged as cross-agent dependencies
4. Validate payload against [schemas/handoff.v1.json](../../schemas/handoff.v1.json).
5. Write to `projects/{p}/_handoffs/d365-ce-to-{target}-{feature}.handoff.json`.
6. Update CE spec §10 traceability matrix with `<!-- split-emitted: {target} -->` markers.
7. Update `.workflow.json`: `currentStates` += `SPEC_SPLIT`; record in history.
8. Print pointer to handoff manifests so the user can pass them to the target agents' `/spec --source handoff` (or `New-Feature.ps1` + `/spec` flow).

## Output

- `projects/{p}/_handoffs/d365-ce-to-{target}-{feature}.handoff.json` (one per target)
- `.workflow.json` updated
